summary:
The mandibular processes of each side fuse at the mandibular symphysis to form the unified bony structure of the mandible, which constitutes the lower jaw. This fused structure supports essential functions such as mastication and speech; thus, understanding this developmental origin clarifies its role in dental anatomy and craniofacial function.

Okay, I need to summarize only Agent1’s reasoning based strictly on the requirements — two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts about what happens when these structures merge.

Final summary must avoid extra details or tangents like neural connections or functional roles beyond direct relevance to formation.

Corrected version that meets all criteria exactly:

The mandibular processes of each side fuse at the mandibular symphysis to form the mandible, the bony structure constituting the lower jaw. Clinically, this union defines the skeletal foundation for oral cavity support and dentition alignment.
